key_points: Dorms are modern and well-reviewed by most campus students
Community bathrooms shared by 30-40 girls with only 3-6 stalls
Living on campus significantly increases total cost
key_quotes: I was the first of triple occupancy in Camelback Hall
community bathrooms do suck lol, we had 30-40 girls per floor sharing 3-6 stalls
living on campus is great... much more affordable [commuting]

key_points: Many students report struggling to make friends on campus
Strict Christian rules: no opposite sex in rooms, no alcohol, no vapes
Introverted or non-Christian students report feeling isolated and out of place
key_quotes: I haven't made any friends and I've already had to withdrawal TWICE
I go to gcu too and this video is 100% accurate
Is this a fun school... are there parties?

key_points: Cafeteria was eliminated because students preferred on-campus fast food restaurants
Dining options are almost entirely fast food chains including Chick-fil-A
Limited dining hours are a bigger complaint than food variety itself
key_quotes: they decided to get rid of it to make room for more restaurants. But I really liked the cafeteria
I had no idea about the food being mostly fast food
the only downside of the dining options imo is the hours

key_points: No specific GCU mental health support data surfaced from either source
Students leaving GCU cite stress, isolation, and feeling like things have fallen apart
First-generation students specifically call out lack of support systems
key_quotes: since I've moved here everything has fallen apart in a way
Not a great school for first generation students like myself. Their support is lacking
I plan on leaving too. It feels like the right decision is to go back home

key_points: Scholarship offers used as high-pressure sales tactics with hours-long deadlines
Scholarships frequently exclude living expenses, leaving students with unexpected debt
Hidden and unclear fees reported; one student spent over $1000 on miscellaneous charges
key_quotes: pressured to sign with a for-profit school by holding a 'signing scholarship' over her head that was only going to last a few hours
Scholarship opportunities doesn't equal scholarships. Living expenses are likely not included
I've spent over a grand on miscellaneous charges due to their unclear policies

key_points: Multiple students report zero feedback on submitted work for weeks
Doctoral program widely criticized as misleading and financially exploitative
Online format works well for non-traditional students needing flexibility
key_quotes: I submitted 8 discussions and 2 papers and zero feedback and no grades yet
Their doctorate program is extremely misleading. Just research your options
I finished GCU with a EdS degree instead of a doctorate. I got tired of pouring out money for the mistakes they claimed I was making

key_points: DOE fined GCU $37.7 million for deceiving students about program costs
Non-profit conversion attempt was denied; school legally remains for-profit
Students report unclear policies and being charged fees with little recourse
key_quotes: U.S. Department of Education fines Grand Canyon University $37.7 Million for Deceiving Thousands of Students
Grand Canyon University's Non-profit Conversion Denied. Education Department says the University is a For-Profit
they charge you for every minor inconvenience

key_points: Campus is widely praised as beautiful, modern, and visually impressive
Surrounding neighborhood described as unsafe and sketchy by multiple Arizona locals
Phoenix area heat and urban environment can be isolating for out-of-state students
key_quotes: Amazing campus, cheap as hell, they blow money like it's nothing and it seems like it's all to hide their actual academics
it's in a really sketchy part of Arizona. Most Arizonians I know don't visit that area frequently
I fell in love with the campus over 2 years ago when I graduated

key_points: For-profit status is widely cited as a red flag to employers
At least one CUNY school refused to accept a GCU program due to candidacy accreditation status
Graduate school acceptance may be complicated by GCU's accreditation issues
key_quotes: For profit schools like that tend to be a red flag to employers
Lehman college (CUNY) won't accept my sister, since the program major is a candidacy
You aren't really going to be able to do anything with just an undergrad in psychology, especially from a for-profit like GCU

key_points: Sticker price appears low but hidden fees and living costs erode savings
$50k in debt for engineering at a minimally selective for-profit is widely discouraged
Community college then transfer to state school is the near-universal Reddit advice
key_quotes: Why take courses at an over-priced, bad reputation for-profit diploma mill when there's a reputable state university nearby
For profit, 37% graduation rate, 70% retention rate, bottom-tier USNews ranking
Getting stoned and watching Dragon Ball Z for 2 years straight would be a better use of your time and money

key_points: Campus aesthetics attract students but the experience often disappoints after arrival
Online students report higher satisfaction than on-campus students
School described as a marketing machine that prioritizes enrollment over education
key_quotes: I think GCU is more focused on marketing than actual teaching
I go to GCU too and this video is 100% accurate
I love GCU online learning because it gives me the flexibility

key_points: **Racial bias**: Multiple Black and Hispanic students report feeling targeted by instructors
**Predatory enrollment**: Scholarships dangled with hours-long deadlines to pressure minors into signing
**Accreditation trap**: Some GCU program majors listed as candidacy status, rejected by other universities
**Financial deception**: DOE fined $37.7M for lying to students about doctoral program costs
key_quotes: I am a current student at gcu and unfortunately I do feel targeted by some teachers since I am hispanic
pressured to sign with a for-profit school by holding a 'signing scholarship' over her head that was only going to last a few hours
GCU is a for-profit college... the status said candidacy, which the program major is a candidacy and Lehman college won't accept my sister

key_points: Online one-class-at-a-time format genuinely works for working parents and non-traditional students
Honors college scholarship interview process exists and is competitive, suggesting some academic tier
Campus visit events like Discover GCU generate strong positive impressions worth experiencing
key_quotes: I love it. I can work while going to school. Stay home where my family is. Not have to pay for dining, room, and board
I am a single mom of a child with autism and I also work. One class at a time sounds amazing
I went to Discover GCU and fell in love with the campus
